key_insight: The paper shows that fully relativistic NC-SOC DFT can be scaled to exascale by pairing a residual-tolerant eigensolver with mixed precision and compressed MPI communication.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical high-fidelity electronic-structure simulations for magnetic and heavy-element materials while cutting compute, memory traffic, and communication overhead.
method: Residual-based Chebyshev filtered subspace iteration (R-ChFSI) for sparse generalized eigenproblems
Mixed-precision computation with block floating-point compressed MPI communication
Matrix-free finite-element Poisson solver plus communication-efficient band partitioning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved time-to-solution, excellent exascale scaling, compression ratios above 4x, and fully relativistic pseudopotential DFT simulations of up to 100,000 electrons.
watch_item: Accuracy and robustness depend on preserving solver convergence under inexact matrix-vector products and compression, so implementation must carefully manage error tolerances.
paper_title: Towards exascale fully relativistic pseudopotential density functional theory calculations enabled by mixed-precision computation and compressed-communication using residual based subspace iteration
